steps.title: Set off the league name
steps.content: “The Hanseatic League” renames the powerful mercantile alliance, so it needs punctuation that can introduce an appositive. The sentence then moves to another independent clause beginning with “a loose confederation…, it has been described…”

steps.title: Separate the full clauses
steps.content: A introduces the appositive but leaves the following independent clause joined by only a comma. C separates the first clause from its appositive too early. D creates a comma splice. A dash sets off the appositive, and a semicolon separates the independent clauses. The answer is B.
